text: So, he's finally done it. My fellow Americans, this is Liberation Day. In a few moments, I will sign a historic executive order instituting reciprocal tariffs on countries throughout the world. Donald Trump's plan is to make America wealthy again. He wants to return to the late 19th century when America had no income tax and state spending was funded by tariff revenue instead. The problem is that this is a fantasy. Americans were poorer back then, and tariffs will hurt the economy. Stock markets around the world are reacting badly, and he could hit his country's economy into a recession. So, as you can see here, if Donald Trump fully implements his tariffs, America's tariff rate will return to levels not seen in more than 100 years. The president announced a blanket 10% tariff on all of America's trading partners. He also announced reciprocal tariffs on many countries, which are meant to reflect unfairness in how they deal with America. These reciprocal tariffs are based on arbitrary and spurious calculations. They assume that any trade deficit that America runs with a trading partner is unfair, when in fact American consumers benefit hugely from trade. According to the calculations, the EU would get a reciprocal charge of 20%, China 34%, and Vietnam 46%. And these tariff rates explain why a company like Apple has suffered in the stock market ever since the announcement. Apple does a lot of its production in China and some in Vietnam. As the cost of imports rises, prices for businesses and households in America will go up as well. It's effectively a big tax increase for American consumers. The rest of the world, particularly Asia, is especially vulnerable. Unable to sell to America's markets much, Chinese exporters will have to find new markets. But domestic consumption has been weak for a long time. What happens next?
